q: The thermodynamics concerns the relationships between heat and other forms of energy.
Free electrons can carry electricity, not heat.
Heat added to a system is considered positive.
Copper is a better thermal conductor than aluminium.
Radiation can be transferred through a vacuum.
In an adiabatic process, the pressure of the system remains constant.
The efficiency of a heat engine depends on the temperature of the reservoirs.
Gasoline engines are external combustion engines.
There can be net heat transfer between the two systems at the same temperature.
In an isochoric process, the volume of the system remains constant.
Wood is a good thermal conductor.
The first law of thermodynamics is a statement of the conservation of energy.
During the intake stroke of a diesel engine, only air is drawn into the cylinder.
A blackbody is an object that absorbs all radiation that falls on it.
The hotter fluid is denser than the cooler fluid.
Internal energy is the sum of the kinetic and potential energies of the molecules in a system.
Convection is a process by which heat is transferred from one place to another by the actual motion of the fluid.
A refrigerator is a heat engine operating in reverse.
Metals have many free electrons.
If a teaspoon is put into a hot cup of tea, the spoon handle becomes hot.
Materials that are good electric conductors are also good heat conductors.
No heat engine can be 100% efficient.
An isothermal process occurs at constant temperature.
The surroundings of a system are everything outside the system.
The work done is equal to the change in total mechanical energy.
Rubber is a good thermal conductor.
Heat can flow spontaneously from a cold body to a hot body.
Metals are generally very good conductors of heat.
A high value for thermal conductivity indicates the material is a good thermal insulator.
Silver is a better thermal conductor than gold.
Work done on a system is considered positive in thermodynamics.
In fluids, heat is mainly transferred by conduction.
The second law of thermodynamics states that entropy of an isolated system always increases.
In liquids and gases, a much larger quantity of heat is carried by the motion of the fluid itself.
All bodies at a temperature above absolute zero emit radiation.
Aluminum is a better thermal conductor than iron.
In a gasoline engine, the spark plug ignites the fuel-air mixture.
Heat flux density is proportional to the magnitude of the temperature gradient.
A thermodynamic system is a collection of objects that can be considered as a whole.
The compression ratio of a diesel engine is higher than that of a gasoline engine.
There can be net heat transfer between the two systems at different temperatures.
An adiabatic process involves no heat transfer into or out of the system.
During the intake stroke of a gasoline engine, only air is drawn into the cylinder.
The Carnot cycle consists of two isothermal and two adiabatic processes.
Some heat may be transferred by conduction in liquids and gases.
Materials that are good electric conductors have high values of thermal conductivity.
A heat engine is a device that converts heat into work.
The work done by a gas during expansion is equal to the area under the P-V curve.
Styrofoam is a good thermal conductor.
A blackbody is a perfect emitter of radiation.
